figures.caption: TABLE I. Architectural configurations of DeepOPiraKAN.
FIG. 4. Solution accuracy for the imaginary part of the first eight QNMs in the lm = 21 case, shown under the same conditions as Fig. 3.

FIG. 5. Mean PDE loss LPDE for the lmn = 211 mode as a function of spin interval and evaluation resolution. The left panel shows the dependence of LPDE on the number of evaluation steps for different spin intervals, while the right panel displays its variation with spin interval at fixed evaluation steps.

FIG. 1. The Physics Informed Deep Operator Network with Residual Adaptive KAN (DeepOPiraKAN) Architecture. (a) Physics Informed Residual Adaptive Kolmogorov Arnold Network (PiraKAN): In the current model, input coordinates are first projected into a high dimensional feature space with random Fourier embedding, then passes through N blocks of residual adaptive KAN. Each block consists of three KAN layers, gated by two linear dense layers, and adaptively short connected at the end (controlled by a trainable parameter α). (b) The multi-branch DeepONet of the current work to solve QNM equations. The radial and angular coordinates serve as input coordinates of two separate trunk nets, which output the base solution. To achieve parameterized solving of QNM, the ODE parameters pass through multi-branch nets, condition the trunk net outputs, and give rise to the final solution. Boundary conditions are imposed as hard constraints on the network outputs.

FIG. 2. Relative accuracy of the first eight QNMs for the lm = 20 case across the full spin range obtained with DeepOPiraKAN. The left panel shows the relative error in the real part of the QNM frequencies, while the right panel shows the corresponding error in the imaginary part. Relative errors are evaluated with respect to benchmark values computed using the Leaver’s method.

FIG. 3. Solution accuracy for the real part of the first eight QNMs in the lm = 21 case obtained with DeepOPiraKAN, illustrating the effect of evaluation resolution. The left panel shows results obtained using 2 × 103 evaluation steps, while the right panel shows results obtained using 4× 103 evaluation steps.
